1. Quality & consistency

Auto verdict from VR + parameter railing + teleseismic (USGS nodal-plane) agreement. This is the single accept/review/reject signal.

3. Source model (uniform Okada)

Focal mechanism vs USGS (left→right: USGS, uniform, slip-weighted). On each beachball the green solid curve is the chosen fault plane and the red-dashed curve its auxiliary (conjugate) plane. Parameter table: initial start · search bounds · optimum · posterior median (railed rows red).

USGS vs inverted — parameters & depth

parameterUSGS NP1 / hypopygbis invertedΔ (model−USGS)
Mw5.206.42+1.22
Strike (°)29245-144
Dip (°)393-36
Rake (°)-54-50+3
Depth (km)7.8
hypocentre		0.4
top 0.1 · bottom 0.6		-7.4
centroid−hypo

Depth: GBIS fault depth is the up-dip (top) edge; centroid and bottom are derived from inverted Width & dip. The USGS depth is the hypocentre (nucleation), which typically sits near the fault bottom — so model bottom ≈ USGS hypo while the slip centroid is shallower.

4. Distributed slip

Distributed slip on the surface-extended fault; data/model/residual fit shown for every track (joint multi-track inversion).

distributed slip not run.

5. Static stress drop

Average static stress drop. Left: uniform (Eshelby + geometric) vs distributed estimates. Right: Δσ vs smoothing — a flat plateau over the data-resolvable range means Δσ is data-constrained, not a smoothing artefact.

modelr_eq (km)area (km²)slip (m)Δσ (MPa)
uniform Okada (Eshelby r=√(LW/π))4.151.63.2635.68
uniform Okada (geometric μ·D/W)——3.2614.67

Average static stress drop. Eshelby circular-crack Δσ=(7/16)·M0/r³ is the standard single estimate; geometric μ·D/W is a shape-dependent check. Typical tectonic earthquakes: 1–10 MPa (intraplate often higher).
